ogTitle: Codjatotine vs Cold Harbour: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Codjatotine and Cold Harbour.
heading: Codjatotine vs Cold Harbour — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Codjatotine, Western Australia and Cold Harbour, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Codjatotine has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Cold Harbour (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Codjatotine or Cold Harbour safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Codjatotine has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Cold Harbour scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Codjatotine scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Cold Harbour scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Codjatotine or Cold Harbour?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Codjatotine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
